questions.context:
The table shows the lengths (mm) of 60 leaves collected from a tree.

| Length (mm)   | Frequency |
|---------------|-----------|
| 10 < l ≤ 20  | 6         |
| 20 < l ≤ 30  | 15        |
| 30 < l ≤ 40  | 22        |
| 40 < l ≤ 60  | 12        |
| 60 < l ≤ 80  | 5         |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Calculate the frequency density for each class and hence describe how a histogram of this data should be drawn.

markScheme.desc: fd = frequency ÷ class width used for at least one correct

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out an estimate for the mean leaf length.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Explain why this is only an estimate of the mean.
parts.correctAnswer: The exact values within each class are unknown; midpoints are used as approximations

markScheme.desc: Individual values not known; midpoints used as approximation oe

parts.label: d
parts.questionText:
A second sample has the same mean but a larger standard deviation.
Describe how the histogram for the second sample would differ.
parts.correctAnswer: The histogram would be more spread out / flatter with taller bars at the extremes

markScheme.desc: More spread out / wider distribution / bars at extremes would be taller oe

questions.context:
A histogram is drawn for the ages (years) of 80 members of a gym.
The bars have the following heights (frequency density):

| Age group      | Freq. density |
|----------------|---------------|
| 15 < a ≤ 25   | 1.8           |
| 25 < a ≤ 35   | 2.4           |
| 35 < a ≤ 45   | 2.0           |
| 45 < a ≤ 65   | 0.9           |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Show that the total number of members is 80.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out the number of members aged over 45.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Work out the probability that a randomly selected member is aged 35 or under.

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Work out an estimate for the mean age of gym members.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
A histogram has a bar for the class 20 < x ≤ 30 with frequency density 4.5, and a bar for 30 < x ≤ 50 with frequency density 2.
Work out how many times greater the frequency in 20 < x ≤ 30 is compared to 30 < x ≤ 50.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Give a reason why, for this histogram, a student would be wrong to say '4.5 > 2 so more values fall in 20 < x ≤ 30 than in 30 < x ≤ 50'.
parts.correctAnswer: You cannot compare frequency densities directly unless class widths are equal; frequency = fd × class width

markScheme.desc: Cannot compare fd directly as class widths differ; must compare areas / frequency = fd × class width oe
